title: service-accounts — Eloquent observers

handles: creating → populate ULID prefix `svc_`
creating → validate `name` via valid_service_account_name (unique per application_id)
creating → refuse when application_id does not exist OR is disabled
creating → when tenant_id is populated, refuse when tenant.application_id != row.application_id (APPLICATION_MISMATCH_ON_TENANT)
creating → when signer_kid is populated, refuse when kid does not exist in auth::jwt_signing_keys OR is disabled OR is bound to a different application_id
creating → when signer_kid is null, request a fresh kid via auth::JwtSigningKeyProvisioner::provision($applicationId) and set signer_kid to the returned value
creating → compute initial status: 'pending' (never used yet)
creating → stamp secret_rotated_at with now() so age-based rotation checks have a baseline
creating → fire ServiceAccountCreated
updating (name dirty) → refuse when the new name exists on another SA within the same application_id
updating (application_id dirty) → REFUSE unconditionally (SERVICE_ACCOUNT_IMMUTABLE_FIELD) — application_id is immutable
updating (tenant_id dirty) → REFUSE unconditionally (SERVICE_ACCOUNT_IMMUTABLE_FIELD) — tenant_id is immutable after creation
updating (signer_kid dirty) → REFUSE unconditionally (SERVICE_ACCOUNT_IMMUTABLE_FIELD) — kid rotation goes through auth::JwtSigningKeyRotator, not a direct PATCH
updating (secret_hash dirty) → stamp secret_rotated_at with now(); refresh expires_at = now() + INTERVAL '$ttl days' when ttl is provided; recompute status; fire ServiceAccountSecretRotated
updating (is_enabled transition false → true) → refuse when expires_at IS NOT NULL AND expires_at < now() (caller must rotate first); recompute status; fire ServiceAccountEnabled
updating (is_enabled transition true → false) → recompute status = 'disabled'; fire ServiceAccountDisabled
updating (expires_at dirty) → refuse when the proposed value < now() (cannot shorten below the present); recompute status

deleting (soft) → emit `stackra.service_account.deleted` metric; fire ServiceAccountDeleted(erasure_phase='soft_delete'); write compliance-audit entry with actor + reason; also flip is_enabled to false in the same transaction
restoring → refuse when retention hold has elapsed (row must be gone by then); clear deleted_at; write audit entry
forceDeleted → fire ServiceAccountDeleted(erasure_phase='hard_delete') AFTER the row is gone (via afterCommit); anonymise audit rows referencing this SA to `[REDACTED-SERVICE-ACCOUNT]`

notes: The observer is registered by ServiceAccountServiceProvider::boot(). It runs BEFORE HasActivityLog + HasAudit hooks so audit trails see the fully-normalised row.
Every `refuse` case throws a subclass of `Stackra\ServiceAccount\Exceptions\ServiceAccountViolationException` — mapped to the errors in `errors.json`.
The `updating (secret_hash dirty)` branch NEVER touches password_history (SA secrets don't need reuse prevention). It's a clean overwrite — no history retained.

The `updating (is_enabled)` transitions run cache invalidation via the `svc:lookup:id:{id}` + `svc:signer_kid:{kid}` entries in the same transaction — pre-emptive invalidation prevents a race where a request in flight sees stale state.
